source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure Automatic Backups and Retention Policies are configured

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure Automatic Backups and Retention Policies are configured
input_row.description: Backups help protect your data from accidental loss or database failure. With Amazon Aurora, you can turn on automatic backups and specify a retention period. The backups include a daily snapshot of the entire DB instance and transaction logs.
input_row.rationale: The individual logs into their account and chooses their database once selected they can modify the backup settings. To have the database being backed up automatically the individual is encouraged to select from 1 to 35 days. This ensures that the file is being saved automatically and can prevent it from accidental loss. This ensures that the individual can restore their files quickly in the event of a data loss. Impact: It would result in having the files protected and being able to retrieve those files in the event of an accidental loss.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign in to AWS Management Console • If you do not already have an AWS account, you will need to create one at https://aws.amazon.com. 2. Navigate to Amazon RDS Dashboard • Navigate to the RDS service once logged in to the AWS Management Console. • You can find this under the Database category. 3. Choose your Aurora DB instance • In the RDS Dashboard, click on Databases. • Then click on the name of your Aurora DB instance. 4. Check or modify the backup settings • In the Details section, find the Backup section. Here, you can see if automatic backups are enabled (the Backup retention period is more than 0 days) and when the backup window is.  o To modify these settings, click Modify. o In the Backup section of the Modify DB instance screen, you can change the Backup retention period and the Backup window. o The retention period can be between 1 and 35 days. To disable automatic backups, set the retention period to 0 days. 5. Apply the changes • Scroll to the bottom and choose when to apply the changes. You can apply them immediately or schedule them for the next maintenance window. • Then, click Continue and Modify DB Instance.
input_row.remediation: This is important because it would allow the user to automatically save their files and instantly have access to their files when needed. References: 1. https://aws.amazon.com/products/databases/
input_row.impact: It would result in having the files protected and being able to retrieve those files in the event of an accidental loss.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control pertains to Amazon Aurora, which is part of the AWS RDS service.
- Backup settings such as automatic backups and retention periods are standard properties for RDS instances.
- AWS APIs, specifically the boto3 SDK and AWS CLI, can be used to query these settings.
- AWS service documentation references standard methods to retrieve and modify backup settings for RDS.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_rds_instance_backup_retention_period_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls/boto3 SDK methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('rds')` to interact with the RDS service.
  - Call `describe_db_instances()` to list all RDS instances and their configurations.
  - Retrieve the `BackupRetentionPeriod` from the response to check if it is greater than 0, indicating that automatic backups are enabled.
  
- **AWS CLI commands:**
  - Use `aws rds describe-db-instances` to obtain the list of RDS instances and their properties.
  - Filter the output to check the `BackupRetentionPeriod` for each instance.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Iterate over all RDS instances within the AWS account and region where the audit is conducted.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Validate that the `BackupRetentionPeriod` for each RDS instance is set between 1 and 35 days.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** The `BackupRetentionPeriod` for an RDS instance is 1-35 days.
  - **Fail:** The `BackupRetentionPeriod` for an RDS instance is set to 0 or is not configured.

By leveraging the AWS APIs and tools, this control can be reliably checked through an automated process ensuring that backup and retention policies for RDS instances comply with the specified requirements.
